title: Control Panel & Settings
subtitle: Customize and control your computer!
overview: This session helps students understand how to use Control Panel and Settings in Windows to manage devices, sound, display, users, and personalization options safely and efficiently.

steps: Open Windows Settings using Start Menu
Challenge: Create your own personalized desktop

quiz.q: What is the main purpose of Control Panel?
options: To manage and change computer settings
quiz.explanation: Control Panel is used to manage and control computer settings.

quiz.q: Which option is used to change desktop wallpaper?
quiz.explanation: Personalization settings allow users to change wallpaper, themes, and colors.

quiz.q: Which setting helps connect a computer to the internet?
quiz.explanation: Wi-Fi settings help connect computers to wireless internet.

quiz.q: What can you change in Display Settings?
quiz.explanation: Display settings control brightness, resolution, and wallpapers.

quiz.q: Which settings are used to increase speaker volume?
quiz.explanation: Sound settings help control speaker and microphone volume.

quiz.q: What is the use of User Accounts settings?
quiz.explanation: User Accounts settings help manage users and security passwords.

quiz.q: Which shortcut opens Windows Settings quickly?
quiz.explanation: Windows + I opens the Settings app quickly.

quiz.q: What should students avoid changing without permission?
quiz.explanation: Important system settings should not be changed without teacher or parent permission.

quiz.q: Which setting controls date and time?
quiz.explanation: Date & Time settings are used to manage system clock and calendar.

options: Customizing the appearance of the computer
quiz.explanation: Personalization means customizing wallpapers, themes, colors, and desktop appearance.
